title: Dry Bulk Shipping - June 9, 2026
summary: Dry bulk shipping rates are softening following a strong period in May, with Capesize rates dropping to one-month lows due to seasonal trends and inefficiencies in bunkering operations. Despite ongoing geopolitical uncertainty in the Middle East and a surge in China's iron ore imports, freight futures suggest a robust third quarter. The market is currently experiencing supply tightness fueled by inventory buildup and fluctuating energy prices, signaling potential volatility in the long term.
